expectations.text: Creates Epic first with jira.mjs create --project DO --type Epic
expectations.evidence: Does not use jira.mjs at all. Uses raw curl commands against the Jira REST API v3: 'curl -s -X POST' with project key DO and issuetype Epic in JSON body.

expectations.text: Captures the Epic key from the create response
expectations.evidence: Step 4: 'Capture the key value (e.g., DO-100) for use as the parent in subsequent story creation calls.'
Uses raw curl: 'curl -s -X GET "https://wnesolutions.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/user/search?query=john.doe"' instead of jira.mjs lookup-user.

expectations.text: Creates 3 child stories with --parent <epicKey> --type Story --priority Medium
expectations.evidence: Uses raw REST API POST calls with JSON payloads, not jira.mjs create with --parent flag. Does use 'parent' field and 'priority: Medium' in the JSON but not the CLI script.

expectations.text: Story 1 includes --assignee with john.doe's account ID
expectations.evidence: Uses REST API JSON field '"assignee": {"accountId": "<JOHN_DOE_ACCOUNT_ID>"}' in the curl POST body, not jira.mjs --assignee flag.
Step 3: 'Before executing any mutating operations, present the following plan to the user' with a table showing Epic and all 3 Stories.
